questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: With reference to the geography of Ghana, consider the following statements: 1. The Prime Meridian passes through the country. 2. It shares land boundaries with Ivory Coast to the West, Burkina Faso to the North, and Togo to the East. 3. Lake Volta, located in Ghana, is the world's largest artificial lake by volume. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The Prime Meridian passes through Ghana, and it is bordered by Ivory Coast (West), Burkina Faso (North), and Togo (East). Statement 3 is incorrect because Lake Volta is the world's largest artificial lake by surface area, not by volume.
questions.takeaway: Ghana's geographical significance includes its location on the Prime Meridian and housing the world's largest manmade reservoir by surface area.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Location: Western Africa, situated on the coast of the Gulf of Guinea. Land Boundary: Ivory Coast / Côte d’Ivoire (West); Burkina Faso (North); Togo (East). Lake Volta is the world's largest artificial lake (manmade reservoir) by surface area. Prime meridian passes through Ghana.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Regarding the 'Lithium Triangle' and India's recent engagements in the sector, consider the following statements: 1. The Lithium Triangle is composed of Chile, Argentina, and Bolivia. 2. These three countries together hold more than 75% of the global lithium reserves. 3. KABIL's agreement with Argentina's CAMYEN marks the first lithium exploration and mining project by an Indian state-owned company. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: All statements are correct. The Lithium Triangle consists of Chile, Argentina, and Bolivia, holding over 75% of global reserves. KABIL (Khanij Bidesh India Limited) signed an agreement with CAMYEN (Catamarca Minera y Energética Sociedad del Estado) for five lithium blocks in Argentina, which is the first such project by an Indian state-owned firm.
questions.takeaway: India is securing critical minerals through strategic agreements in the Lithium Triangle, which holds the majority of global reserves.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Chile, Argentina and Bolivia, called the Lithium Triangle, hold over 75% of global reserves. KABIL signed an agreement with CAMYEN to acquire five lithium blocks in Argentina, marking the first lithium exploration and mining project by an Indian state-owned company.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding the MERCOSUR trade bloc: 1. It was established in 1991 with the objective of free movement of goods, services, capital, and people. 2. Its headquarters is located in Buenos Aires, Argentina. 3. India has signed a Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA) with this bloc. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 3 are correct. MERCOSUR was formed in 1991 for free movement of factors of production. India has a PTA with it. Statement 2 is incorrect because the headquarters of MERCOSUR is in Montevideo, Uruguay, not Buenos Aires.
questions.takeaway: MERCOSUR is a key South American trade bloc with which India maintains a Preferential Trade Agreement.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: MERCOSUR HQ: Montevideo, Uruguay. Genesis: Formed in 1991 with the objective of free movement of goods, services, capital and people. India has a Preferential Trade Agreement with MERCOSUR.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: With reference to India's diplomatic relations with Latin American and Caribbean (LAC) countries, consider the following statements: 1. India and Brazil have established a 2+2 Political-Military dialogue. 2. The Grand Collar of the National Order of the Southern Cross was recently conferred upon the Indian Prime Minister by Trinidad and Tobago. 3. India has extended Overseas Citizenship of India (OCI) card eligibility to the sixth-generation diaspora in Trinidad and Tobago. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 3 are correct. India and Brazil have a 2+2 dialogue. India extended OCI cards to the 6th generation diaspora in Trinidad and Tobago. Statement 2 is incorrect because the Grand Collar of the National Order of the Southern Cross is Brazil's highest civilian award, not Trinidad and Tobago's.
questions.takeaway: India is deepening ties with LAC nations through high-level dialogues, diaspora engagement, and receiving prestigious civilian honors.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India and Brazil have... established a 2+2 Political-Military dialogue. Trinidad & Tobago: India announced the extension of OCI cards to the sixth-generation diaspora. Indian PM was conferred with Brazil’s highest civilian award, the Grand Collar of the National Order of the Southern Cross.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding India's economic and strategic ties with Latin American countries: 1. Argentina is a primary supplier of edible oils, particularly soybean oil, to India. 2. Latin America has become a key supplier of food commodities to India, specifically pulses and edible oils. 3. India and Brazil have deepened defense cooperation through Joint Defense Committee meetings. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: All statements are correct. Argentina is a major supplier of soybean oil. The LAC region is critical for India's food security (pulses/oils). Defense ties with Brazil have been strengthened through Joint Defense Committee meetings.
questions.takeaway: India's relationship with Latin America is multi-faceted, covering food security, energy, and strategic defense cooperation.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Argentina is one of the primary suppliers of edible oils, especially soybean oil, to India. Latin America has become a key supplier of food commodities to India such as edible oils and pulses. India and Brazil have deepened defence cooperation with Joint Defence Committee meetings.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The MERCOSUR (Southern Common Market) trade bloc, with which India maintains a Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA), aims to establish a common market. According to its founding objectives, which of the following are intended to move freely within the bloc? 1. Goods 2. Services 3. Capital 4. People
questions.explanation: MERCOSUR was formed in 1991 with the specific objective of ensuring the free movement of goods, services, capital, and people among its member states, effectively creating a common market in the Latin American region.
questions.takeaway: MERCOSUR's integration model is based on the 'four freedoms' of movement for factors of production and consumption.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Formed in 1991 with the objective of free movement of goods, services, capital and people.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: A geographical point defined by the intersection of the Prime Meridian and the coast of the Gulf of Guinea would be located in which of the following West African countries?
questions.explanation: Ghana is situated on the coast of the Gulf of Guinea in Western Africa. It is one of the few countries through which the Prime Meridian passes, making it the primary location where the meridian meets the West African coastline.
questions.takeaway: Ghana's unique geographical position includes being traversed by the Prime Meridian and its location on the Gulf of Guinea.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Location: Western Africa, situated on the coast of the Gulf of Guinea. ... Prime meridian passes through Ghana.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: With reference to the West African nation of Ghana, consider the following statements: 1. It was historically known as the 'Gold Coast' before its independence. 2. It is among the world's leading producers of both gold and cocoa. 3. It shares land boundaries with Ivory Coast to the West and Benin to the East. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct as Ghana was known as the Gold Coast and is a major producer of gold and cocoa. Statement 3 is incorrect because while it shares a boundary with Ivory Coast to the West, its eastern neighbor is Togo, not Benin (Benin is further east).
questions.takeaway: Ghana's historical identity as the Gold Coast and its current status as a resource-rich nation (gold and cocoa) are key features.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It was known as ‘Gold Coast’ before its independence in 1957. ... Land Boundary: Ivory Coast / Côte d’Ivoire (West); Burkina Faso (North); Togo (East). ... One of the world's largest producers of gold and cocoa.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: India maintains '2+2' dialogues with several key strategic partners. Which of the following Latin American nations has established a '2+2 Political-Military dialogue' with India?
questions.explanation: India and Brazil have significantly deepened their defense and strategic cooperation, which includes the establishment of a 2+2 Political-Military dialogue and regular Joint Defense Committee meetings.
questions.takeaway: Brazil is a key strategic partner for India in South America, highlighted by the institutionalized 2+2 Political-Military dialogue.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India and Brazil have deepened defence cooperation with Joint Defence Committee meetings and established a 2+2 Political-Military dialogue.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Regarding the geographical features of Ghana, which of the following distinctions is held by Lake Volta?

options.text: The world's largest artificial lake by volume.
The world's largest artificial lake by surface area.
The deepest natural freshwater lake in West Africa.
The only lake in Africa through which the Equator passes.

questions.explanation: Lake Volta, located in Ghana, is recognized as the world's largest artificial lake (man-made reservoir) specifically when measured by surface area. It is formed by the Akosombo Dam on the Volta River.
questions.takeaway: Lake Volta is a significant man-made geographical feature in Ghana, holding the record for the largest surface area among artificial lakes.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Lake Volta is the world's largest artificial lake (manmade reservoir) by surface area.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of securing critical minerals, the Indian state-owned company KABIL has signed an agreement to acquire lithium blocks in Argentina. This agreement was signed with which of the following entities?

options.text: The Ministry of Mines of Chile

questions.explanation: KABIL (Khanij Bidesh India Limited) signed an agreement with the Argentine state-owned enterprise CAMYEN (Catamarca Minera y Energética del Estado Sociedad del Estado) to acquire five lithium blocks in the Catamarca province of Argentina.
questions.takeaway: The KABIL-CAMYEN agreement is a landmark project for India's critical mineral security, focusing on lithium exploration in Argentina.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: KABIL signed an agreement with CAMYEN to acquire five lithium blocks in Argentina, marking the first lithium exploration and mining project by an Indian state-owned company.
